tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58273,"title":"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in TypeSpec migration (v1.6.0-beta.1)","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` **Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57069) by `@live1206` ## What Changed The package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec, upgrading the API version to 2026-03-01. This added several new custom resource types and supporting models: - `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` — manage AI Foundry Projects under a Cognitive Services account - `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` / `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` — manage capability hosts scoped to a project - `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` — data model shared by account- and project-scoped capability hosts - `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` / `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` — new model types - `CognitiveServicesExtensions` — new extension methods on `ArmClient` and `SubscriptionResource` ## Gaps Found - **README "Key concepts" section is empty of service content** — it links to the generic Azure SDK docs page but does not describe any of the resource types in this package (accounts, projects, capability hosts, connections, deployments, etc.) - **README "Examples" section has no inline code snippets** — it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) The `tests/Samples/` folder contains 35+ generated sample files (including new ones for `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, `CognitiveServicesConnection`) but none are wired into the README via `Snippet:` placeholders - **CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is incomplete** — only records "Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01" without listing the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, capability host data models, new extension methods) added by the migration 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to enumerate new resource types: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01. - Added `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` to manage AI Foundry Projects nested under a Cognitive Services account. - Added `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` and `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` to manage capability hosts scoped to a project. - Added `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` as the shared data model for both account-level and project-level capability hosts. - Added `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` and `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` model types. - Added `GetCognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` extension method on `ArmClient`. ### Breaking Changes ### Bugs Fixed ### Other Changes ``` #### 1b. Edit `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Replace the generic **Key concepts** section with service-specific content, and add `Snippet:` placeholders to the **Examples** section. See Step 2 below for the snippet details. Replace the "Key concepts" section body: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### CognitiveServicesAccountResource Represents a top-level Azure Cognitive Services account. Accounts hold AI models, deployments, connections, projects, and capability hosts. ### CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource Represents a model deployment within an account. Use this to create, update, or delete deployments of AI models. ### CognitiveServicesProjectResource Represents an AI Foundry Project nested within a Cognitive Services account. Projects can have their own connections and capability hosts. ### CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource / CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource Represents a capability host attached either to an account or to a project. Capability hosts define the AI capabilities (e.g., storage, vector search) available to the parent resource. ### Thread safety We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline]((azure.github.io/redacted) This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads. ### Additional concepts [Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) | [Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) | [Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) | [Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) | [Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) | [Mocking]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) | [Client lifetime]((devblogs.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the "Examples" section body: ```markdown ## Examples The following examples show common operations using the management library. ### List all Cognitive Services accounts in a subscription ``` ``` ### Create or update an account ``` ``` ### Create a project within an account ``` ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The repository uses a snippet-generation tool: write sample code in a test file with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`, add a placeholder in the README, then run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject the code. #### Snippet 1 — List accounts: `CognitiveServices_GetAccounts` **Test file to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample_ReadmeSnippets.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Samples { public partial class Sample_ReadmeSnippets { [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task GetAccountsAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_GetAccounts TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); await foreach (CognitiveServicesAccountResource account in subscription.GetCognitiveServicesAccountsAsync()) { Console.WriteLine(account.Data.Name); } #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task CreateAccountAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts(); CognitiveServicesAccountData accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")) { Kind = "OpenAI", Sku = new Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models.CognitiveServicesSku("S0"), }; ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync( Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, "myAccount", accountData); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created account: {account.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task CreateProjectAsync() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = await resourceGroup .GetCognitiveServicesAccountAsync("myAccount"); CognitiveServicesProjectCollection projects = account.GetCognitiveServicesProjects(); CognitiveServicesProjectData projectData = new CognitiveServicesProjectData( new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation operation = await projects.CreateOrUpdateAsync( Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, "myProject", projectData); CognitiveServicesProjectResource project = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created project: {project.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` **README placeholders** — the three `Snippet:` fences added in Step 1b will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after the test file is committed. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` is missing or incomplete in these sections: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1b) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline snippets | Add `Snippet:` placeholders (see Step 1b and Step 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None | | Next steps | ✅ Present | None | | Contributing | ✅ Present | None | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — regenerates public API listing files (no new public types are being added by the docs changes, so this is a no-op validation) 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` — ensures consistent code formatting in the new sample file ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24555405679/agentic_workflow) · ● 472K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58273","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T08:29:23Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:29:23Z"}

ArmOperation operation = await projects.CreateOrUpdateAsync( Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, "myProject", projectData); CognitiveServicesProjectResource project = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created project: {project.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` **README placeholders** — the three `Snippet:` fences added in Step 1b will be filled in by `Update-Snippets.ps1` after the test file is committed. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` is missing or incomplete in these sections: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1b) | | Examples | ⚠️ No inline snippets | Add `Snippet:` placeholders (see Step 1b and Step 2) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None | | Next steps | ✅ Present | None | | Contributing | ✅ Present | None | Reference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — regenerates public API listing files (no new public types are being added by the docs changes, so this is a no-op validation) 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` — ensures consistent code formatting in the new sample file ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24555405679/agentic_workflow) · ● 472K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58273","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T08:29:23Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:29:23Z"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-generated documentation gap issue for `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` v1.6.0-beta.1, requesting README and CHANGELOG updates following the TypeSpec migration that added new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProjectResource`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource`, etc.).

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` (v1.6.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** README Key Concepts & Examples sections; CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` entry
**Scenarios:**
- README "Key concepts" section is generic and lacks descriptions of the new resource types
- README "Examples" section has no inline code snippets (only external links)
- CHANGELOG 1.6.0-beta.1 only records the API version upgrade, omitting new resource types

**Root ask:** Update CHANGELOG and README with service-specific content and `Snippet:` placeholders; add a sample file for the three new scenarios (list accounts, create account, create project).
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue triggered by an automated workflow (commit [REDACTED] / PR #57069). No runtime bug or functional regression is involved.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57069 to confirm which new types (`CognitiveServicesProjectResource`, capability host resources) were introduced by the TypeSpec migration
2. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` to enumerate new resource types in the `1.6.0-beta.1` section
3. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Key Concepts and Examples sections; add `Snippet:` placeholders
4. Create a sample file under `tests/Samples/` and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` to inject code into the README
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to verify compilation
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — package name begins with `Azure.ResourceManager.*`, clearly a management-plane library
- **Service:** `Cognitive Services` — issue title and body explicitly name `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` and service directory `sdk/cognitiveservices/`
- **Confidence:** High — package name unambiguously maps to a single category and service; `Cognitive Services` label (color #e99695) confirmed to exist in the repository
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) — catch-all for all management-plane issues; no more-specific `%Cognitive Services %Mgmt` entry exists in CODEOWNERS
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978`; no `needs-team-attention` label added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan encountered `%CodeGen %Mgmt` (line 1221), `%SQL %Mgmt` and other service-specific mgmt entries (lines 1146–1164), `%ARM - Templates %Mgmt` (line 936), `%ARM %Mgmt` (lines 915, 918), `%ARM - Core %Mgmt` (line 921) — all required an additional label not present on this issue; `%Mgmt` at line 903 was the first full match
</details>
